Controls.Caption:
Use  this form to import a user list directly into either EPC or DBP. 
If users will have access to both, we recommend that you import the user list directly into EPC and then use the Synchronization with EPC application to import the list into DBP.
Controls.Tooltip:
The expected file is an Excel document (.xlsx) with the user list on Sheet1.

The column headers expected on the first row are:

UserName, FirstName, MiddleName, LastName, Email, PhoneNumber, Password, Roles(s), Department, Epcadmin, Modeler, Portal, Active, Code, AltCode, Cell, Address, Description

Controls.Caption: An exported user list from the Organization Management application can be used as a template.
Controls.Tooltip:
The column headers on the first row are:

Name, ID, Active, UserCode, Email, Tel, Address, Login, AlternativeCode, FirstName, LastName.

Controls.Caption:
For a new role, you will need to input the corresponding department. For an existing role, the department is not required.
The system will disregard a blank or null value under the "Login" and/or "Username" columns.
• If you want to enable EPCAdmin, Modeler, and/or Portal for the user, input “Yes” in the relevant fields. If you do not want to enable these permissions, instead leave the columns blank.
• In the "Active" column, input "Yes" to mark the item as active. To mark it as inactive, leave the corresponding row blank.
• In the Org. Management Excel file, the "Active" column will instead contain "True" or "False" as values. These values will still be accepted by the system.
If the list is imported directly into EPC, only the users will be added to the system. You will need to manually link/create the corresponding roles and departments for each user.
Download Excel Template
Controls.Tooltip: Click on the file to download

Validators.Error: Please enter a value for "Upload File with Data"
